rulings.Case Title: M. DATTANI CREDIT TRUST VS. MR. C'S LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 5, 2024 line 1. PLAINTIFF M. DATTANI CREDIT TRUST AMENDED NOTICE OF APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER AND ORDER FOR ISSUANCE OF WRIT OF ATTACHMENT is continued to February 29, 2024. No later than February 23, 2024 Defendant to provide evidence in support of the financial statement, e.g. lease, cancelled checks for rental payment, car insurance invoices, utility bills, credit card statements, banks statements, pay stubs, etc. Plaintiff may file a response no later than February 27, 2024. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: RUSS BUILDING VENTURE LLC VS. TOLTEC CAPITAL LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order That Requests For Admissions Propounded On David Cohen, Set One, Be Deemed Admitted And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $770.00; And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Herein
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 5, 2024 line 6. PLAINTIFF RUSS BUILDING VENTURE LLC NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER THAT REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS PROPOUNDED ON DAVID COHEN, SET ONE, BE DEEMED ADMITTED AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $770.00; AND MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES HEREIN is OFF CALENDAR, insufficient notice. E-service on January 30, 2024 does not provide for additional 2 business days for notice. Personal service on January 31, 2024 is purportedly made per CCP 1011 by delivery to law office. There is no attorney of record in this action for this defendant. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ALI A. AMINI ET AL VS. ANDREW LINTON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 5, 2024 line 8. DEFENDANT ANDREW LINTON, ANGELINA GREEP DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint states a cause of action for unlawful detainer and is not uncertain. The factual issue whether Plaintiff's counsel is a debt collector within the meaning of FDCPA is not appropriate for determination on a demurrer. Exemption from TPA 2019 is sufficiently stated. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ALI A. AMINI ET AL VS. ANDREW LINTON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 5, 2024 line 8. DEFENDANT ANDREW LINTON, ANGELINA GREEP MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to seek daily damages through entry of judgment only. Plaintiff fails to provide any authority for the proposition that daily damages are available beyond judgment "for each day defendants continue in possession." =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
